---
title: "4.7 Wrapper Classes"
subtitle: "Data Collections"
format:
  clean-revealjs:
    slide-number: true
    css: unit4-shared.css
lang: zh-CN
---

## Course Directory

### Return to the course outline

[← Back to AP CSA / 返回课程目录](../../index.html)

## Wrapper Classes

### Wrapper classes give primitive values an object form

The textbook focuses on `Integer` and `Double`.

::: {.tight-list}
- a <span class="term">wrapper class</span> encloses a primitive value in an object
- `Integer` wraps `int`
- `Double` wraps `double`
- wrapper objects matter when APIs or collections require objects
:::

## Creating `Integer` and `Double` Objects

### Wrapped values behave like objects, not raw primitives

```java
Integer i = 2;
Double d = 3.5;
```

Retained ideas:

::: {.tight-list}
- wrapper classes live in `java.lang`
- wrapper objects are <span class="term">immutable</span>
- the classes also expose useful constants like min and max values
:::

## Overflow and Underflow

### Integer ranges are fixed

The textbook keeps one concrete consequence of fixed-size integer storage:

::: {.tight-list}
- adding `1` to `Integer.MAX_VALUE` rolls over
- subtracting `1` from `Integer.MIN_VALUE` also wraps around
- these constants are also useful when initializing min/max searches
:::

## Autoboxing and Unboxing

### Java can convert automatically between primitives and wrappers

::: {.compare-grid}
::: {.soft-box}
**Autoboxing**

- primitive to wrapper
- for example `int` to `Integer`
- happens when an object is required
:::
::: {.soft-box}
**Unboxing**

- wrapper to primitive
- for example `Integer` to `int`
- happens when a primitive value is required
:::
:::

This becomes especially important with `ArrayList`, which stores objects rather than primitive types.

## Parsing Strings to Numbers

### Wrapper classes also provide conversion utilities

```java
int n = Integer.parseInt(text);
double x = Double.parseDouble(text);
```

Retained points:

::: {.tight-list}
- file and scanner input often begins as strings
- parsing turns those strings into values that can be compared or used in arithmetic
- these methods are part of the AP quick-reference expectations
:::

## Classroom Tasks

### Practice worth keeping

Retained classroom work for this topic:

::: {.tight-list}
- identify what wrapper classes do
- distinguish immutable wrapper objects from primitive values
- explain autoboxing and unboxing
- use `Integer.parseInt` and `Double.parseDouble` correctly
- connect wrappers to collections like `ArrayList`
- <span class="term">4.7.4 Coding Challenge: Pokemon Speed</span>
:::

## Classroom Check

### A strong answer should...

::: {.tight-list}
- define a wrapper class
- explain why `Integer` and `Double` are objects
- distinguish autoboxing from unboxing
- explain when parsing is needed
- explain why wrapper classes matter for collections
:::

## End

### Return to the course outline

[← Back to AP CSA / 返回课程目录](../../index.html)
